title: Crocanthemum carolinianum
taxonomy: Crocanthemum carolinianum is classified as a member of the family Cistaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as rockrose. This classification is based on its shared characteristics with other species within the family. The genus Crocanthemum is a small group of flowering plants native to North America, and C. carolinianum is one of its species. The family Cistaceae is part of the larger order Rosales, which also includes other flowering plants such as roses and strawberries. The classification of C. carolinianum helps scientists understand its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
uses: Crocanthemum carolinianum, also known as Carolina rose or frostweed, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves, stems, and roots are said to have anti-inflammatory properties and are used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for wound care and preventing infection. In addition, Crocanthemum carolinianum is used to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea and indigestion. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.

intro: Crocanthemum carolinianum, also known as Carolina rose, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southeastern United States. The health benefits of Crocanthemum carolinianum include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in the treatment of various skin conditions, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Crocanthemum carolinianum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as topical creams and ointments used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
